role: This file is the surface-side registry and live DOM scanner that exposes spotlightable UI targets to the brain via a global window.surfacePointables() function.
capabilities: Exposes a curated catalog of pointable controls/instruments as opaque tokens paired with ui:// addresses and human labels
Installs a singleton global function surfacePointables() for fork-brain-core to read at send-time
Filters the curated catalog to targets currently present in the DOM via [data-ui-ref] selectors
Auto-discovers additional pointable elements that declare data-ui-ref and data-point-label inline
Merges curated and auto-discovered lists with curated entries winning on duplicate addresses
Generates opaque per-call auto-N tokens so parseable addresses never reach the brain
Escapes double quotes and backslashes when building CSS attribute selectors
substance_why: It implements a real catalog, DOM presence filtering, auto-discovery with deduplication, opaque token generation, and a one-time global installer.
interactions_missing: Sector pointables are excluded because the spotlight visual is currently suppressed
Operator-facing labels are AI-supplied drafts awaiting final review/Tim's steer
No cleanup or uninstall of the global window.surfacePointables() function
No reactive updates beyond caller polling; no mutation observer or cache invalidation
No validation or normalization of data-ui-ref or data-point-label values
performance_risks: document.querySelectorAll('[data-ui-ref][data-point-label]') scans the whole DOM on every call
document.querySelector is run once per curated item on every call
Auto-discovered list is unbounded and could grow large if many elements declare pointability
No throttling, debouncing, or caching of the live scan result
reuse_why: The address-free brain contract, curated catalog, and auto-discovery seam are worth preserving, but the global installer, hardcoded list, and per-call full-DOM scan should be rebuilt.
decomposition_hints: Split the curated catalog data from the live DOM scanner
Split the global window bridge installer from the registry logic
Split auto-discovery heuristics from curated token/label management
Extract CSS selector escaping into a shared utility
